title: Author cross-platform archive-hint.cjs Stop-hook script reading events.jsonl
description: Create packages/cli/templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs — single .cjs Node script (zero npm deps), shebang #!/usr/bin/env node, requires only node:fs + node:path. Resolves projectRoot via process.cwd(), reads .fabric/events.jsonl with ENOENT-tolerance + partial-tail tolerance (matches event-ledger.ts:52-100 readEventLedger semantics), counts plan_context events since last knowledge_proposed event, applies threshold logic: 5 plan_contexts since last knowledge_proposed OR 24h elapsed since last knowledge_proposed (whichever first). On threshold-trigger emits stdout JSON {decision:'block', reason:'<reminder text mentioning fabric-archive skill>'} with exit 0 (matches existing fabric-init-reminder.cjs:12-18 pattern). On non-trigger silently exit 0. Exports `main(env, stdio)` for in-process testability per signal-handler.test.ts policy. Authors a contract test in packages/cli/__tests__/archive-hint.test.ts.

files.target: Node CJS Stop-hook script with main(env, stdio) export
files.change: Shebang + require node:fs (existsSync, readFileSync) + require node:path (join). Top-of-file CONSTANTS block: FABRIC_DIR='.fabric', EVENT_LEDGER_FILE='events.jsonl', THRESHOLD_PLAN_CONTEXTS=5, THRESHOLD_HOURS=24. Function readLedger(projectRoot): resolve path, return [] on ENOENT, readFileSync utf8, split on /\r?\n/, drop last partial line if missing trailing newline, JSON.parse per-line swallowing per-line errors, return event array. Function decide(events, now): walk events from tail, find last knowledge_proposed, count plan_context events after it (or all if none), check delta-hours from last knowledge_proposed; return null on no-trigger or {decision:'block', reason:`已积累 N 次 plan_context 调用且距上次 knowledge_proposed 超过 Mh — 建议调用 fabric-archive skill 抽取本次会话的知识。`}. Function main({cwd, now}, {stdout}): events=readLedger(cwd); result=decide(events, now); if result: stdout.write(JSON.stringify(result)); always exit 0. module.exports={main, readLedger, decide, CONSTANTS} for testability. Bottom: if (require.main===module) main({cwd:process.cwd(), now:new Date()}, {stdout:process.stdout}); process.exit(0);

Import { main, decide, readLedger } from '../templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs'. Tests: (1) no-trigger silence — empty events.jsonl, decide returns null. (2) threshold-trigger by count — 6 plan_context events after last knowledge_proposed, decide returns block. (3) threshold-trigger by hours — last knowledge_proposed 25h ago, 1 plan_context, decide returns block. (4) partial-tail tolerance — events.jsonl with last line missing newline, readLedger drops it without throwing. (5) ENOENT — non-existent .fabric/events.jsonl, readLedger returns []. (6) corrupt JSON line — single bad line interleaved with good lines, readLedger drops bad lines. (7) main wiring — call main({cwd: tempDir, now: fixed-time}, {stdout: spy}) with fixture; assert spy.write called with valid JSON parsing to {decision:'block', reason:string}.

implementation: Read templates/claude-hooks/fabric-init-reminder.cjs and templates/codex-hooks/fabric-stop-reminder.cjs to confirm shebang + require shape + stdout JSON pattern
Read packages/server/src/services/event-ledger.ts:52-100 readEventLedger to mirror split/parse semantics (regex /\r?\n/, ENOENT->[], partial-tail drop)
Read packages/server/__tests__/signal-handler.test.ts:1-14 to confirm in-process test policy
Author archive-hint.cjs with module.exports for testability — keep require.main check for CLI invocation parity
Hardcode CONSTANTS at top with comment 'DRY violation accepted — script runs without node_modules access in user repos'

Implement decide(events, now) function: reverse-walk to find last knowledge_proposed; if found, count plan_context after it AND compute hours-elapsed; if not found, count all plan_context AND treat hours-elapsed as Infinity; trigger if (count >= 5) OR (hours >= 24); return reason string with both metrics for transparency
Author archive-hint.test.ts with 7 test cases per files spec
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test archive-hint` and iterate until green
Commit: `feat(cli): add archive-hint.cjs Stop-hook script with threshold logic`

reference.pattern: CommonJS Node hook script with stdout JSON output and in-process test export
reference.examples: Mirror fabric-init-reminder.cjs L1-3 (shebang + requires) and L12-18 (stdout JSON write + exit 0). Mirror event-ledger.ts:52-100 split/parse semantics WITHOUT importing — duplicate the constants and the regex. Mirror signal-handler.test.ts in-process invocation pattern (no child_process.spawn).

criteria: archive-hint.cjs uses zero npm dependencies (only node:fs + node:path) — verifiable by grep -n require
Script outputs stdout JSON {decision:'block', reason:string} on threshold-trigger (NOT stderr+exit2)
Script exits 0 on both trigger and no-trigger paths
ENOENT on .fabric/events.jsonl results in silent exit (no output, exit 0)
Partial-tail line in events.jsonl is dropped silently without throwing
Threshold logic: trigger when (plan_context count since last knowledge_proposed >= 5) OR (hours since last knowledge_proposed >= 24); both metrics evaluated independently
Reason text mentions 'fabric-archive skill' so Claude/Codex display directs user to right action
All 7 vitest cases pass via in-process import (no child_process.spawn in CI)
Per-file coverage ≥90% on archive-hint.cjs (verified by TASK-008)

rationale.chosen_approach: Single .cjs script with module.exports for in-process testing, hardcoded constants, ENOENT-tolerant ledger reader
alternatives_considered: Three separate scripts per client — rejected per discussion.md L451 (single script works for both Claude Stop and Codex Stop)
child_process.spawn-based test — rejected per signal-handler.test.ts:1-14 explicit policy
stderr+exit2 output — rejected per existing repo pattern (fabric-init-reminder.cjs:12-18 uses stdout+exit0)
.js extension — rejected per existing .cjs precedent across all template hook scripts
decision_factors: Hook runs in user repo without node_modules — must be vendor-free
Existing precedent dictates output shape and file extension
In-process test pattern allows coverage measurement (counts toward ≥90% gate)
rationale.tradeoffs: Hardcoded constants (FABRIC_DIR, EVENT_LEDGER_FILE) duplicate _shared.ts values — accepted DRY violation because hook cannot import server package; documented inline with comment

manual_checks: Run `node packages/cli/templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs` from a project with no .fabric/ → confirm silent exit 0
Manually seed .fabric/events.jsonl with 6 plan_context events and run script → confirm stdout JSON {decision:'block', reason:...}
Inspect script source: confirm shebang on line 1, require statements only on lines 2-3 (matches existing template files)
success_metrics: Coverage ≥90% on archive-hint.cjs (decide() and readLedger() and main() all hit)
Script source file is < 100 lines (matches existing template script length norm)
